artPrompt: A dramatic moment as a single square day torn from existence is sucked upward into a swirling vortex of flame and smoke above the rioting town square, its blank face crumbling into glowing embers that scatter against the night, leaving a dark ragged hole in the air where it once was. Goblins below shield their eyes. Mono-red palette of flame orange, ember red, and black smoke, surreal calendrical erasure, composed wide in a 4:3 landscape frame, rendered as a vibrant gouache storybook illustration — saturated matte color, simplified flat-ish shapes, soft painterly edges, decorative.
name: Exile Target Tuesday
text:
Exile target Tuesday. It does not come back. None of them do.
If it is currently not Tuesday, Exile Target Tuesday resolves anyway out of spite.
Scry your soul, then draw a conclusion. (You may not like it.)
The days that remain reshuffle themselves. The week is now six days long and everyone agrees not to talk about it.
flavorText: Monday looked for its friend and found only a Thursday-shaped hole.
